summary:
Claim: Independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during meiosis I.  
Evidence: - Homologous chromosomes (one maternal, one paternal) separate during anaphase I of meiosis I. - This separation results in each resulting secondary spermatocyte receiving a unique combination of maternal and paternal chromosomes.  
Answer: B

This summary maintains all critical biological concepts—specific timing (anaphase I), mechanism (separation of homologous pairs), outcome (genetic diversity via random combinations)—while eliminating redundancy and preserving clarity for clinical or educational contexts. It aligns precisely with standard developmental biology principles regarding gametogenesis and genetic variation formation in male germ cells.
